description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a distinctive triangular ridge running along its length, giving it a wedge-like profile when viewed from the ends. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible pitting and natural stone texture, suggesting it is made from a solid piece of quarried stone. The block is predominantly a pale, off-white color with some subtle discoloration and weathering marks. One of the shorter faces features a visible inscription or marking, possibly Roman numerals, etched or carved into the stone. The block's sides are straight and flat, while the top is sharply angled, making it suitable for use as a barrier or edging in urban or historical architectural settings. Its substantial mass and solid construction indicate it is designed to be a durable, immovable obstacle or boundary element.
description: A pale, wedge-shaped stone block with a rough, weathered surface and a prominent ridge along its top. One end features an inscribed marking, adding a historical or functional detail.
description_view_0: The block is seen from an end, highlighting the triangular ridge and visible inscription on the face.
description_view_1: A side view showing the long, flat surface and the rough, pitted stone texture.
description_view_2: Another end view, similar to view 0, but without visible markings; the triangular profile is clear.
description_view_3: The opposite side view, displaying the flat length and subtle discoloration on the stone.
attribute_location_context: historic urban street or plaza as a boundary or curb element
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set in a shallow trench
attribute_weathering_pattern: The stone shows signs of surface pitting, minor discoloration, and some edge wear, indicating exposure to the elements over time.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible inscription, likely Roman numerals, on one end face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Aligned in a row or series to form a continuous barrier or curb.
